summary: Slovak past tense uses the l-participle + auxiliary 'byť'. The participle agrees in gender/number (-l, -la, -lo, -li). The auxiliary is omitted in 3rd person. Negation adds 'ne-' to the participle. Reflexive 'sa/si' follows the auxiliary. Aspect matters: imperfective = ongoing/habitual, perfective = completed.

rules.title: Past Tense Formation
rules.text: Past participle (l-form) + auxiliary 'byť'. Participle endings: -l (masc sg), -la (fem sg), -lo (neuter sg), -li (plural). Auxiliary omitted in 3rd person.

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs - 'učiť sa' (to learn)
rules.text: Reflexive 'sa/si' comes after the auxiliary (or after participle in 3rd person).

rules.title: Negation
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the participle. Auxiliary stays the same.
examples: Neučil som sa – I didn't learn

rules.title: Aspect in Past Tense
rules.text: Imperfective = ongoing/habitual ('what was happening'). Perfective = completed ('what happened').

rules.title: Key Irregular Past Participles
examples: Bol som doma – I was at home
Išla som do školy – I went to school
Jedli sme pizzu – We ate pizza
Mohol som prísť – I could come
Vedel som odpoveď – I knew the answer

vocab.en: to stand up, to get up
to look forward to, to be glad
to sign up, to log in
